channel.title: Internship & Graduate — Research & Polling
channel.description: Job listings for Research & Polling roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Quality Analyst
item.description: Title: Quality Analyst Location: Parsippany NJ Who We Are: Ipsos is one of the world’s largest research companies and currently the only one primarily managed by researchers, ranking as a #1 full-service research organization for four consecutive years. With over 75 different data-driven solutions, and presence in 90 markets, Ipsos brings together research, implementation, methodological, and subject-matter experts from around the world, combining thematic and technical experts to deliver top-quality research and insights. Simply speaking, we help the biggest companies solve some of their biggest problems, serving more than 5000 clients across the globe by providing research, data, and insights on their target markets. And, we’re proud to share we’ve received our Great Place to Work Certification for 2022! About Innovation: The Innovation Service Line guides clients throughout the innovation journey as they develop and launch new or improved products and services spanning a range of verticals. We help our clients develop the initial idea for a product/service, ensure the fully articulated description of the product/service resonates with consumers, confirm that prototypes of the product/service properly deliver on consumer expectations, and finetune the full mix prior to launch. This is accomplished using best-in-class services, including idea screening, concept testing, product testing, price and line optimization, package testing and volumetric forecasting. What you can expect to be doing: Quality control of consumer interviews and survey data Ensuring the quality and consistency of responses collected through our online surveys and face-to-face interviews conducted in our Retail Labs Reviewing responses to open-ended questions to identify inconsistent or unreliable interviews Monitoring survey quotas to ensure samples meet client expectations Conducting random checks on survey results to detect potential inconsistencies Reviewing videos collected during Mobile Eye Tracking studies to ensure the data is usable and of sufficient quality. Monitoring the execution of fieldwork protocols Ensuring that fieldwork protocols defined with our partners are properly implemented during face-to-face studies: Visiting our own facilities or partner locations when necessary to verify that all fieldwork materials and installations (shelves, survey equipment, etc.) are compliant with project requirements. Ensuring teams have been properly briefed and that interviews are conducted according to the agreed protocols. Training or retraining field teams on our protocols when necessary Monitoring and reporting on internal quality standards Supporting compliance with PRS IN VIVO internal quality processes: Ensuring Insights and Operations teams are familiar with PRS IN VIVO processes and able to apply them correctly. Reporting internal compliance status to global teams This might be the job for you if you have: Located within reasonable distance of the Parsippany, NJ office. Flexible and willing to travel to our various field locations. Highly organized and detail-oriented Able to work independently Proactive mindset Good command of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! In accordance with NY/CO/CA/WA law, the estimated base salary range for this role is $48,000 to $52,000. Your final base salary will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors which may include but are not limited to location, work experience, skills, knowledge, education and/or certifications. What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit : Why Work at Ipsos | US Why Work at Ipsos | CA Inclusive Workplace Commitment Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-BM1 #LI-Remote
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Qualitative Associate
item.description: Title: Qualitative Associate Location: Parsippany NJ Who We Are: Ipsos is one of the world’s largest research companies and currently the only one primarily managed by researchers, ranking as a #1 full-service research organization for four consecutive years. With over 75 different data-driven solutions, and presence in 90 markets, Ipsos brings together research, implementation, methodological, and subject-matter experts from around the world, combining thematic and technical experts to deliver top-quality research and insights. Simply speaking, we help the biggest companies solve some of their biggest problems, serving more than 5000 clients across the globe by providing research, data, and insights on their target markets. And, we’re proud to share we’ve received our Great Place to Work Certification for 2022! About PRS IN VIVO: PRS IN VIVO is a global leader in market research, specializing in shopper research, packaging tests, and product testing for over 30 years. PRS IN VIVO is part of the Ipsos Group. With regional operations in the US, France, the UK, Germany, Switzerland, Italy, China, and Singapore, PRS IN VIVO supports clients worldwide. While many research companies focus on fast, low-cost solutions, PRS IN VIVO remains committed to high-quality, customized research leveraging behavioral science expertise. What makes this role important at Ipsos? As a Qual PM at PRS IN VIVO, you will be part of a global team of project managers and ensure operational excellence and seamless project delivery in close partnership with the Insights team. What you can expect to be doing: Project setup & Recruitment management Manage project handover from Insights team lead Develop and manage screeners (including approvals and translations if required) Coordinate and oversee recruitment Build and track timelines, ensuring key milestones are met Fieldwork & Logistics coordination Book and liaise with venues, recruiters, and external suppliers Coordinate moderators and translators where required Organise fieldwork logistics (materials, stimulus preparation, shelf setups, online links, streaming, platforms, etc.) Monitor respondent grids and ensure quality control during fieldwork Supplier & Budget management Act as the main operational contact for suppliers and partners Ensure smooth collaboration between all stakeholders Manage supplier invoicing and track project costs Backroom Assistance Attend some studies (Virtual & in-person) Assist with backroom management (arranging meals / responding to client requests) Note-taking Growth Opportunities Discussion Guide training and development Assist Insights lead with report writing This might be the job for you if you have: 2–3 years’ experience in qualitative research project management (agency or client-side) Strong understanding of recruitment and fieldwork processes Excellent organisational skills and attention to detail Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously Confident communicator with suppliers and internal stakeholders If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we encourage all who feel they might be a fit for the opportunity to apply. We may consider a variety of backgrounds for a particular role and are also committed to considering candidates for available positions throughout our organization, not just the one you’re applying to! In accordance with NY/CO/CA/WA law, the estimated base salary range for this role is $65,000 to $70,000. Your final base salary will be determined based on several non-discriminatory factors which may include but are not limited to location, work experience, skills, knowledge, education and/or certifications. What’s in it for you: At Ipsos you’ll experience opportunities for Career Development, an exceptional benefits package (including generous PTO, healthcare plans, wellness benefits), a flexible workplace policy, and a strong collaborative culture. To find out more about all the great reasons to work at Ipsos, how we’re making an impact around the world, and more about our benefits and employee programs, please visit : Why Work at Ipsos | US Why Work at Ipsos | CA Inclusive Workplace Commitment Ipsos recognizes the necessity of building an inclusive culture that values each employee’s individuality and diverse perspectives. For more than 40 years, our mission has been to generate and analyze data about society, markets, brands, and behaviors to provide our clients with the insights that elevate their understanding of the world. This could not be fulfilled without Ipsos’ diverse employees who compile and analyze this data—they are the essence of who we are and what we do. We are committed to providing equal opportunity to all employees, creating an environment that promotes inclusion, and enabling employees from all walks of life to flourish. Ipsos encourages our employees to act in a respectful and responsible manner, in line with code of best practices concerning diversity and inclusion, human rights, equality, and civility for every individual. Ipsos is An Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, or any other protected class and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. #LI-BM1 #LI-Hybrid
item.dc:creator: Ipsos

item.title: Project Executive (w/m/d) – trend·test
item.description: Als eines der weltweit führenden Marktforschungsunternehmen geht es bei Ipsos nicht nur um Zahlen. Wir entdecken tiefe Einblicke und bieten klare Handlungsempfehlungen, um unsere Kunden bei wichtigen Entscheidungen zu unterstützen. Unsere breite Palette von Expertisen umfasst alles von Car Clinics, die die Nutzerfreundlichkeit neuer Fahrzeugmodelle analysieren, bis hin zu umfangreichen Healthcare-Studien und der präzisen Analyse politischer Trends. Ob es um User Experience, die Effektivität von TV-Spots oder KI-gestützte Social Media-Analysen geht – wir decken alle Facetten moderner Markt- und Sozialforschung ab. trend·test ist speziell für die telefonische Datenerhebung von Ipsos international tätig. Wir suchen frühstens zum 01.01.2027 eine:n Berufseinsteiger:in oder Quereinsteiger:in als Project Executive (w/m/d) für trend·test am Standort Berlin. Deine Rolle bei uns In unserem Team arbeitest du gemeinsam mit Kolleg:innen für unsere Kunden und bist eigenständig verantwortlich für folgende Aufgaben: Du steuerst CATI-Projekte im nationalen und internationalen Umfeld – unter Beachtung von Qualität, Zeit und Budget. Du organisierst den laufenden Befragungsbetrieb (Markt- und Sozialforschung, kein Sales). Du bewertest, motivierst und betreust Interviewer:innen. Du führst Rekrutierungsmaßnahmen, Informationsveranstaltungen, Bewerbungsgespräche und ganztätige Grundschulungen mit neuen Interviewer:innen durch. Du kümmerst dich um die Prozesse rund um die Weiterentwicklung und Begleitung neuer Interviewer:innen. Du veranstaltest studienbezogene Einweisungen der Interviewer:innen und erstellst die dazugehörige Dokumentation sowie Schulungsunterlagen. Du kontrollierst die Qualität der erhobenen Befragungsdaten. Du unterstützt die Geschäftsleitung bei organisatorischen Aufgaben. Du wirkst an der Weiterentwicklung von Qualitätsstandards mit. Du kommunizierst mit internen und externen Kunden – national und international. Was du mitbringst Du möchtest deine ersten Erfahrungen in der Markt-, Media-, Politik- und Sozialforschung sammeln oder vertiefen und hast großes Interesse an diesen Themen. Dabei bist du eine kommunikationsstarke Persönlichkeit mit sicherem Auftreten gegenüber internen und externen Kunden. Es fällt dir leicht, dich selbst zu motivieren und im Team zu arbeiten. Folgendes solltest du mitbringen: Du denkst analytisch und bringst Kommunikations- sowie Organisationsstärke mit. Du hast ein gutes Verständnis für Zahlen und arbeitest präzise. Du arbeitest äußerst strukturiert, eigenverantwortlich, verlässlich und praxisorientiert. Du verfügst über sehr gute MS-Office-Kenntnisse. Du beherrschst Deutsch auf exzellentem und Englisch auf gutem Niveau. Das bieten wir dir Dich erwartet ein Arbeitsumfeld voller Internationalität und Innovation bei Ipsos. Wir betreuen renommierte Kunden aus fast allen großen Branchen und arbeiten weltweit an spannenden Projekten! Du kannst dich freuen auf: Hybrides Arbeiten Flexible Arbeitszeiten Workation im europäischen Ausland Ipsos Corporate Benefits Zuschuss zum Deutschlandticket Job Rotation Weiterentwicklung & Learning Culture Internationale Zusammenarbeit Events & Standort Community Diversity & Inclusion Corporate Social Responsibility Moderner Arbeitsplatz Klingt spannend? Dann bewirb dich jetzt! Wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbungsunterlagen (Lebenslauf, Zeugnisse und optional ein kurzes Anschreiben) unter Angabe deines frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins und deiner Gehaltsvorstellung.
item.dc:creator: Ipsos
